popular_professors.ranking_basis: Curated from the official MIT EECS people directory, prioritizing current institute-wide academic leaders, named chairs, lab directors, and highly visible faculty with active MIT profile pages.
popular_professors.source_summary: Extracted from official MIT EECS people pages on eecs.mit.edu.

items.name: Daniela Rus
items.department: Electrical Engineering and Computer Science / Artificial Intelligence + Decision-making
items.bio: Director of CSAIL, deputy dean of research for the MIT Schwarzman College of Computing, and Panasonic Professor, according to her official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Regina Barzilay
items.bio: School of Engineering Distinguished Professor of AI and Health on her official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Dina Katabi
items.bio: Thuan (1990) and Nicole Pham Professor on her official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Sangeeta Bhatia
items.bio: John and Dorothy Wilson Professor of EECS, per her official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Hari Balakrishnan
items.bio: Fujitsu Professor in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science on his official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Antonio Torralba
items.bio: Faculty head for AI+D and Delta Electronics Professor, according to his official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Tommi Jaakkola
items.bio: Thomas M. Siebel Distinguished Professor on his official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Armando Solar-Lezama
items.bio: Distinguished Professor of Computing in the MIT Schwarzman College of Computing and professor of EECS on his official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Mohammad Alizadeh
items.bio: Professor in EECS, industry officer, and director of the 6-A MEng Thesis Program according to his official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Leslie Kaelbling
items.bio: Panasonic Professor on her official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Hal Abelson
items.bio: Class of 1922 Professor in computer science and AI+D according to his official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Anant Agarwal
items.bio: CEO of edX and Professor Post-Tenure in EECS, according to his official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Akintunde Akinwande
items.bio: Thomas and Gerd Perkins Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and Professor of EE on his official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Marc A Baldo
items.bio: Director of RLE and Dugald C. Jackson Professor in EE according to his official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Sara Beery
items.bio: Homer A. Burnell Career Development Professor and Assistant Professor in AI+D on her official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Tamara Broderick
items.bio: Associate Professor of EECS in AI+D according to her official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Devavrat Shah
items.bio: Andrew (1956) and Erna Viterbi Professor in AI+D on his official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Stefanie Mueller
items.bio: TIBCO Founders Professor and Associate Professor in CS and EE according to her official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Pulkit Agrawal
items.bio: Associate Professor in AI+D and CS on his official MIT EECS profile.

items.name: Fadel Adib
items.bio: Associate Professor at the Media Lab with a joint appointment in EECS, according to his official MIT EECS profile.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official MIT News and MIT Energy Initiative stories featuring recent MIT graduates and alumni with explicit degree-year evidence where available.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official MIT News and MIT Energy Initiative pages on news.mit.edu.

items.name: Mitali Chowdhury
items.bio: MIT News says Chowdhury graduated in 2024 with a BS in biological engineering plus minors in urban planning and environment and sustainability and now works at a startup advancing sequencing-based diagnostics.
items.description: 2026 Gates Cambridge Scholar and sequencing-diagnostics startup employee.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Christina Kim
items.bio: MIT News says Kim graduated in 2024 after majoring in chemistry and biology and worked as a researcher in women’s health at the Wellcome Sanger Institute.
items.description: 2025 and 2026 Gates Cambridge Scholar; women’s-health researcher.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Abigail Schipper
items.bio: MIT News says Schipper graduated in 2024 with a BS in mechanical engineering and a minor in biology and is pursuing medical-device research for pre-hospital trauma systems.
items.description: 2025 Gates Cambridge Scholar focused on medical devices for low-resource care.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Rachel Zhang
items.bio: MIT News says Zhang graduated from MIT with a BS in physics in 2021, then earned an MS in astronomy at Northwestern and went on to pursue a Cambridge PhD in applied mathematics and theoretical physics.
items.description: 2025 Gates Cambridge Scholar and astrophysics researcher.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Sera Tolgay
items.bio: MIT News says Tolgay earned MIT master’s degrees in city planning and architecture in 2018 and works as an urban planner and hydrologist focused on climate adaptation, infrastructure planning, and water management.
items.description: 2025 Gates Cambridge Scholar; urban planner and hydrologist.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Vikas Enti
items.bio: MIT News says Reframe Systems was co-founded by Vikas Enti SM ’20 and describes him as using lessons from MIT’s System Design and Management program to build robotic microfactories for modular housing.
items.description: Co-founder of Reframe Systems.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Tristan Bepler
items.bio: MIT News says OpenProtein.AI was founded by Tristan Bepler PhD ’20 and highlights his work bringing open-source AI models and protein-engineering tools to researchers.
items.description: Co-founder of OpenProtein.AI.
items.major: PhD program (protein engineering / computational biology context not further specified on teaser)
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Richard Swartwout
items.bio: MIT News says Swartwout earned an SM in 2018 and a PhD in 2021 and co-founded Active Surfaces to commercialize lightweight peel-and-stick solar films.
items.description: Co-founder and CTO of Active Surfaces.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Shiv Bhakta
items.bio: MIT News says Bhakta earned an MBA ’24 and SM ’24 through the Leaders for Global Operations program and co-founded Active Surfaces, where he serves as CEO.
items.description: CEO and co-founder of Active Surfaces.
items.major: Civil and Environmental Engineering / Business (Leaders for Global Operations)
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Sadhana Lolla
items.bio: MIT News identified Lolla as a 2024 MIT senior majoring in computer science, conducting robotics and trustworthy-AI research, and headed to Cambridge for an MPhil in technology policy; this entry treats the 2024 senior profile as a near-term alumni record.
items.description: 2024 Gates Cambridge winner focused on robotics, AI, and technology policy.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school profile covers a current senior or very recent graduate and is being used as a near-term alumni proxy.

items.name: Rupert Li
items.bio: MIT News says Li graduated from MIT in 2024 with a double major in mathematics and computer science, economics, and data science, and earned an MEng in computer science, economics, and data science; he is now pursuing advanced mathematics study and a future PhD in mathematics.
items.description: 2025 P.D. Soros Fellow and future mathematics PhD student.
items.major: Mathematics / Computer Science, Economics, and Data Science
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Jupneet Singh
items.bio: MIT News says Singh graduated from MIT in 2023 with a degree in chemistry and a concentration in history, won a Rhodes Scholarship, and is pursuing medical training with plans for military and public-health service.
items.description: 2025 P.D. Soros Fellow, Rhodes Scholar, and future physician.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Vivian Chinoda
items.bio: MIT News says Chinoda graduated in 2025 with a BS in business analytics and was named a Rhodes Scholar to pursue social data science and public policy at Oxford.
items.description: 2026 Rhodes Scholar focused on social innovation and evidence-based policy.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Alice Hall
items.bio: MIT News says Hall is a senior studying chemical engineering with a minor in Spanish and will pursue engineering research at Oxford focused on sustainable heating and cooling technologies; this entry treats her as a near-term alumni record.
items.description: 2026 Rhodes Scholar focused on clean-energy engineering.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school profile covers a current senior or very recent graduate and is being used as a near-term alumni proxy.

items.name: Sofia Lara
items.bio: MIT News says Lara is a senior majoring in biological engineering with a minor in Spanish and will pursue clinical medicine research at Oxford; this entry treats her as a near-term alumni record.
items.description: 2026 Rhodes Scholar pursuing clinical-medicine research.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school profile covers a current senior or very recent graduate and is being used as a near-term alumni proxy.

items.name: Sophia Wang
items.bio: MIT News says Wang graduated in 2024 with a BS in aerospace engineering and a concentration in the design of highly autonomous systems and will pursue mathematical and theoretical physics plus global governance study at Oxford.
items.description: 2026 Rhodes Scholar with aerospace and physics focus.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Lara Ozkan
items.bio: MIT News says Ozkan is a senior majoring in computer science and molecular biology and was named a 2025 Marshall Scholar to study biological science and AI in the U.K.; this entry treats her as a near-term alumni record.
items.description: 2025 Marshall Scholar focused on women’s health and computational biology.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school profile covers a current senior or very recent graduate and is being used as a near-term alumni proxy.

items.name: Steven Truong
items.bio: MIT News says Truong graduated from MIT in 2020 with bachelor’s degrees in biological engineering and creative writing and is now an MD-PhD student focused on diabetes and metabolic disorders.
items.description: 2025 Knight-Hennessy Scholar and physician-scientist trainee.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official person-specific alumni, spotlight, or news profile with explicit school/program context and substantive narrative detail.

items.name: Akorfa Dagadu
items.bio: MIT News says Dagadu is a senior majoring in chemical-biological engineering, researching plastic-degrading enzyme-polymer systems and building a recycling venture in Ghana; this entry treats her as a near-term alumni record.
items.description: 2027 Schwarzman Scholar and recycling-venture founder.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school profile covers a current senior or very recent graduate and is being used as a near-term alumni proxy.

items.name: Andrii Zahorodnii
items.bio: MIT News says Zahorodnii will graduate in 2025 with a BS and MEng in computer science and cognitive sciences and is using AI and neuroscience research to inform global decision-making; this entry treats him as a near-term alumni record.
items.description: 2026 Schwarzman Scholar with AI and cognitive-science focus.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school profile covers a current senior or very recent graduate and is being used as a near-term alumni proxy.
